criteria.reason: The current code posts native requests to the local OpenAI-compatible proxy, forwards previous_response_id and context_management when present, and the configured proxy root on port 8088 answered health and models probes during verification. This matches the documented Responses API surface for continuation and compaction.

The executor now uses a fresh last_response_id for warm turns, falls back to SQLite-backed cold history when the stored response is stale or absent, and clears broken continuation state on failures. Focused unit coverage proves both warm and cold paths.
checks: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts tests/unit/compact.test.ts

criteria.reason: Server-managed compaction remains available through context_management and proxy support for /v1/responses/compact, while local SQLite summary compaction remains the cold-start fallback. The two mechanisms are now explicit and internally consistent.
checks: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts tests/unit/compact.test.ts

criteria.reason: The repository now includes focused unit and integration coverage for proxy-v2 request payloads, warm/cold continuation selection, and compaction-related behavior, and the current build/lint/typecheck/test commands all passed.
checks: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts tests/unit/compact.test.ts

criteria.reason: ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, spec.md, evidence.md, and the current runtime code all describe the same model: proxy-compatible native responses, fresh-only previous_response_id warm continuation, cold SQLite fallback, and distinct compaction layers.

commands_run: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts tests/unit/compact.test.ts
